description_long: This is a round, earthenware bowl with a wide opening and a slightly irregular rim, featuring three small protruding knobs spaced around its upper edge. The exterior surface is adorned with geometric, zigzag patterns in faded red, brown, and black hues, suggestive of traditional or ancient craftsmanship. The bowl's surface shows signs of age, with worn paint and a matte, slightly rough texture. The interior is plain and unglazed, matching the reddish-brown tone of the exterior clay. The bowl appears hand-formed, with subtle asymmetries and imperfections that add to its character. Its size and shape make it suitable for holding or serving food or other items.
description: A hand-crafted, round earthenware bowl with geometric zigzag patterns in faded red and brown, featuring three small knobs on its rim and a worn, matte finish.
description_view_0: This view highlights the bowl's wide opening, two of the three rim knobs, and the clearest section of the geometric zigzag pattern.
description_view_1: This angle shows the bowl's side with all three rim knobs visible, and the pattern appears more faded and less distinct.
description_view_2: This view focuses on one prominent rim knob and a section of the zigzag pattern that is partially worn away.
description_view_3: This side displays two rim knobs and a more continuous stretch of the geometric pattern, with the bowl's curvature clearly visible.
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for communal or decorative use
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric zigzag patterns in red, brown, and black encircle the exterior surface.
